title: The Icons of Spring 2026
subtitle: Ten Pittsburgh businesses creators kept filming this quarter.
angle: the businesses that earned the most pickup from the city's creators and customers this quarter, across every category we cover. Editorial picks based on real coverage, not announcements.
intro:
Page's Dairy Mart on a Sunday in March. The line outside Pusadee's. The Allegheny tap wall in the Strip, scooped beans and all, sitting through another forty years of foot traffic. These are the ten Pittsburgh small businesses creators kept filming this quarter, and customers kept reviewing, and the city kept saying out loud.

A few things worth flagging. Pusadee's Garden held the top of Pittsburgh's Thai conversation by sitting still and letting the room do the work. Spot of Coffee, the alley shop tucked between Shadyside and East Liberty, posted six times in thirty days and earned more pickup per follower than most cafes earn in a year. Allegheny Coffee and Tea Exchange has been on Penn Avenue since the 1980s and is still posting twelve times a month with reels in the mix. Page's, seventy-five years old in Arlington, dropped a single ice cream truck reveal and set the ceiling for the whole sweets category.

None of this is about budget. None of it is about size. It is about showing up the same way every week, on the feed and at the door. Issue 02 will tell you who held the spot and who climbed in.

items.name: Pusadee's Garden
items.descriptor: Pittsburgh's top Thai room this quarter, carried by a garden creators will travel for.
items.descriptor_highlight: a garden creators will travel for

featured_tiktok.author: mad_i_libs

This Thai restaurant is a gorgeous spot for your next date night or special occasion in Pittsburgh! 💖 

There are so many food and drink options, for meat-eaters and vegans alike, you'll want to come back more than once. 🥂 

Tip: Show up
